entry.title: Re: Data activity log size
entry.content: Please refer to note:  https://me.sap.com/notes/0002781343

entry.title: Re: Condition contract management set different document...
entry.content:
Hi @Hadjolian,

The following links provide further information about condition contract management:

  • 2535889 - CCM - Description of delivered configuration - SAP for Me
  • 2815535 - FAQ: Condition Contract Management by Settlement Management in SAP S/4HANA - SAP for Me
  • Settlement Management | SAP Help Portal

Regarding the purpose of accruals you should read Accruals Processing | SAP Help Portal.

entry.title: Fetch sales order Number and Line item displayed on a PPDS block
entry.content:
Hi All!

We have requirement where we need to change sales order status when we fix the block in PPDS. The issue I am facing is we don't know how to get Sales order No. and Item No. for a order displayed on "Detailed Schedule Planning Dashboard" (SAPAPO/CPDS0).

entry.title: Re: Weekly job SM:DVM_NLS_ANALYSIS_<SID> is failing using...
entry.content: Hi Srinivas, To contact the Private Cloud operation team to request role update, please Log Incident by selecting component XX-HSTOPR.

entry.title: SAP ECC : maximum number of cost centers that we can create for a company code ?
entry.content:
Dear All, 

In SAP ECC, in standard, is there a maximum number of cost centers that we can create for one company code ?

(If it is customizable, where can I find it ?)

Kind regards,

entry.title: Document Information Extraction - XML File Support - Error E30
entry.content:
Hello everyone, 

I am trying to process XML Invoices via Document Information Extraction. According to the documentation, filetype XML is supposed to be supported for invoices. When trying to upload XML Files via the Web-UI, an Error-Message is shown, describing "Error: Files of this filetype can not be processed (Code: E30).

After further inspecting the network traffic, HTTP RC 415 - Unsupported Media Type is returned for Content type text/xml.
Can somebody assist here? Already tried multiple different Instances & XML Files. 

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Weekly job SM:DVM_NLS_ANALYSIS_<SID> is failing using...

entry.content: Hi, Both source(PSA) and destination are same storage location and its embedded EWM

entry.title: Re: How to get registered object information from an ABAP...
entry.content: Your advice was very helpful. It looks like it will be useful in the ABAP code. Thank you. By the way, are there plans to make it possible to obtain this information in the CDS view in the future? I would like to use it in the value help for Application Job parameters if possible, but it seems difficult to do so with the XCO API. If you have any information, please let me know.

entry.title: Re: Disaggregation from Month to Weeks in IBP - Integers...
entry.content:
Hi @KateBrown ,

The simplest way to ensure disaggregation results in integer values only is to change the number of decimals of the Key Figure from the default (6) to 0 decimals. This adjustment ensures that values are disaggregated at the weekly level as whole numbers.

For example, if the monthly forecast is 3 pieces and the month consists of 5 technical weeks, only three weeks will receive a value of 1, while the remaining two will receive 0.

The proportionality of the disaggregation can still be maintained through the time profile level (weekweight column, provided it is added in the KF setup, under Period Weight Factor settings).

For more technical details, refer to SAP documentation: SAP Help Link - decimals

entry.title: un approved invoice in SAP.
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have a scenario in which we need a commercial invoice for custom clearance.

Is it possible to get a sort of invoice (which could be non-approved by accounting) and later once approved by Finance we do a accounting entry?

Delivery > good issue > Un-approved invoice (Which could become approved once approved by finance).

What would be the recommendation, please note, we are not using SAP EWM.

entry.title: Re: S4HC Supplier Operational Evaluation Score Calculation Examples
entry.content:
Hi @Qi_Liu
Below is relevant for app F1661A Supplier Evaluation by Quantity.
In SAP S/4HANA's supplier evaluation framework, items with goods receipt (GR) quantity zero are
excluded from quantity variance calculations due to the system's design logic for performance measurement. Here's the detailed rationale, based on the evaluation on criteria and technical configuratin

What I believe is that above mentioned rule is is wrong and need to be changed
Let me explain it by an example:
A purchaseorder held:
Item line 10: 50 PCE
Item line 20: 80 PCE
GR is made fully (100%) for item line 10

APP F1661A does not include item line 20 in the suplier evaluation score.
The evaluation score is only including line item 10 - as here a GR has been made.
Seen from a broader perspective I think it is a odd calculation and the result for evaluation of that particular supplier is wrong, as I have ordered 2 item lines, but in the score only 1 is included.
I think SAP need to change the variance calculation - it is my view that you cant use the evaluation if you do not included all purchase orderlines in your evaluation

#Supplier Evaluation By Quantity | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: un approved invoice in SAP.
entry.content: You can generate Proforma Invoice (F8) to meet Customs formalities.  What you are expecting is not possible

entry.title: Translation icon is not shown for some of published content in Trainer's bar.
entry.content:
Hello, 

I've updated the translation icon for Trainer's bar in Resource playback for Trainer theme. (Found solution here)

So bar was enhanced successfully, but it turned out that for some published content it was not applied, means, a proper icon disappears. The materials have same published conditions, same delivery space. 

What can be the reason of that behavior and how to fixed it? Please advise. I'm noobie in SEN, must confess

entry.content:
Hi,

Please review below KBAs with  the possible causes of the error and resolution

  • KBA 2910559 - 900-001-6 Error when creating a new Job Requisition

Best regards

Alejandro

entry.title: Re: Could not determine EWM stock data for: Storage Locat...

 I think it should be 8 Stock Transfer with Warehouse Task.

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Notification to HR when Onboardee is in MPH
entry.content:
Hi,

Is it possible to send a notification to HR once onboardee have filled the onboarding step?
After this the onboardee will end up in MPH, but HR is not notified. Is it possible to send a notification or an email? 

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: SF LMS oData API List of instructors
entry.content:
Hi Mark , 

In LMS it is not possible to create API call , but you can consume the available API calls mentioned below in ODATA reference guide.
https://help.sap.com/doc/cd753f29622244df98e335af737b5422/2411/en-US/SF_LRN_Web_Svc_Integ.pdf

As of now we have no API calls available to create/update Instructors records.

If you have requirement to mass create/update instructor records , then you can utilize import data tool. you can download instructor template and create/update instructor records in bulk.

Hope this helps !

Regards,
Prakash

entry.title: Re: How to set validation in CO11N that Qty should not be...
entry.content: Hi, Have you tried BAdI BD_WORKORDER_GOODSMOVT_BKF_GDR? https://api.sap.com/badi/BD_WORKORDER_GOODSMOVT_BKF_GDR

entry.title: Table SWWUSERWI vs FuMo SAP_WAPI_CREATE_WORKLIST
entry.content:
Hi all, 

We are currently using the Function module SAP_WAPI_CREATE_WORKLIST to get the number of workitems for every user, This is however causing performance issues when executed for more than 50 thousand dialog users one by one. We just need a count of How many Work items a User has to process for every user.

Can we use table SWWUSERWI instead ? I have compared various cases, the number of work items returned by the table and the function modules are sometimes different, can we still use this table ?

or is there any other utility which could return the number of work items per user in one Go for all the users.

Thanks for any useful Hints. 

Best regards

Ketan

entry.title: Migrate your data through Migration cockpit.
entry.content:
Dear Team,

 

We are currently utilizing the data migration cockpit template for BP vendor creation and have encountered an issue during the simulation phase. The error message displayed is: "Enter External Number" (Message no: CVIV_UI 6).
In our existing system, we follow a different number range concept, as outlined below:

 

Account Group: XXXX

 

BP Number Range: Internal    NR(XXXX)

 

Vendor Number Range: External NR(XXXX)

 

Additionally, there is no checkbox available for maintaining the same numbers.
Could you please advise whether the migration cockpit is designed to work exclusively with the same number range, or if it can accommodate different number ranges as well?Your guidance on this matter would be greatly appreciated.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Migrate your data through Migration cockpit.
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

As per the help documentation,

The BP ID and supplier ID needs to be identical, With the BP Grouping field on the General Data sheet, you arrange the internal or external number ranges (BP ID and supplier ID needs to be identical) and the supplier account group. It's possible to create a supplier and a one-time supplier.

Kindly refer to the help documentation for the same.

 

Thanks and Regards,
Moumita.

entry.title: Re: SAP ECC : maximum number of cost centers that we can...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

In SAP ECC, there is no hard limit on the number of cost centers that can be created for a company code. However, practical constraints come from:

  1. Performance Considerations : a very high number of cost centers can impact system performance, especially for reporting and data processing.
  2. Organizational Complexity : managing too many cost centers can become difficult in terms of master data maintenance.
  3. Field Lengths and Technical Limits : the cost center field (KOSTL) in SAP is 10 characters long, meaning you need a structured naming convention.
  4. Table Size and Database Limits : cost center data is stored in tables like CSKS (Cost Center Master Data) and COSP/COSS (Cost Center Actual/Plan Costs), which have technical limitations based on your system’s database configuration.

In practical implementations, companies manage thousands of cost centers per company code without issues, provided that proper structuring (e.g., cost center hierarchies) is in place.

I hope this answers your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Could not determine EWM stock data for: Storage Locat...
entry.content: Hi,its Setup 8 Replenish WT

entry.title: SAP Gardener: Cultivating a Thriving Digital Ecosystem 🌱🚀
entry.content:
Imagine an SAP Joule Agent that gardens for you. It maintains your integration flows, improves your BEx queries, looks for the latest Fiori UI elements for your apps. Running in the background, this agent reads through logs and script files in your BTP landscape, and suggests improvements for it.

Our team is working to create this agent. More practical details coming soon...


Welcome to a new era of SAP management—a proactive, behind-the-scenes advisor that nurtures your digital garden to perfection. Imagine having a dedicated tool that not only monitors your integration flows, BW, and Fiori apps but also continuously optimizes, updates, and safeguards them. Let’s explore this powerful concept and see how concrete examples for different SAP domains can transform your digital landscape!

Why Embrace the SAP Gardener? 🌟

  • Proactive Maintenance: Regular health checks prevent issues before they impact your operations.

  • Best Practices: Continuous updates to guidelines, glossaries, and scripts keep your system in top shape.

  • Efficiency & ROI: Automation minimizes manual tasks, reduces downtime, and drives cost savings.

  • Scalability: The concept scales across multiple SAP solutions and industries—from manufacturing to finance.

Concrete Examples of SAP Gardeners

1. Integration Flow Gardener 🌐

Purpose:

  • Manage and optimize integration flows within the SAP Integration Suite.

What It Does:

  • Weekly Health Checks: Automatically scans API integration flows to identify bottlenecks.

  • Performance Optimization: Adjusts settings and prioritizes data flows for maximum efficiency.

  • Security Monitoring: Proactively secures integration points and alerts the team to any vulnerabilities.

Example Scenario:
Imagine your organization handles multiple data integrations between SAP modules. The Integration Flow Gardener runs a weekly diagnostic, detects an inefficient data route slowing down transactions, and automatically reroutes the flow for faster performance—all while logging changes for audit and future reference. 🔄💡
It would leverage the standard APIs : https://api.sap.com/package/CloudIntegrationAPI/odata

2. BW Gardener 📊

Purpose:

  • Ensure SAP BW (Business Warehouse) processes run smoothly and data integrity is maintained.

What It Does:

  • Data Quality Assurance: Continuously checks for inconsistencies in data loads and reporting.

  • Query Performance Tuning: Optimizes complex BEx queries to speed up report generation.

  • Cleanup & Maintenance: Removes redundant data scripts and updates reports to align with new business metrics.

Example Scenario:
Your BW system processes vast amounts of data for monthly financial reports. The BW Gardener identifies an outdated script causing delayed data refreshes and replaces it with an optimized version. This proactive tweak not only speeds up the report generation but also ensures that decision-makers always have access to accurate, real-time data. 🔍📈
The agent would read requests from "RSA1"

3. Fiori Gardener 📱

Purpose:

  • Enhance the performance and user experience of SAP Fiori apps.

What It Does:

  • UI/UX Optimization: Monitors app performance and ensures adherence to Fiori design guidelines.

  • Responsive Testing: Regularly checks apps on various devices to guarantee a consistent user experience.

  • Feedback Integration: Collects user feedback to fine-tune app functionalities and interfaces.

Example Scenario:
A popular Fiori app experiences occasional slow load times during peak hours. The Fiori Gardener performs an analysis, pinpoints the issue to an overloaded data call, and implements a caching mechanism to improve responsiveness. The result? Smoother navigation, happier users, and a more efficient app overall. 📲✨
It would read the repositories of these apps, and SAP BTP Cloud Foundry logs

Key Takeaways 📝

  • Continuous Improvement: Regular monitoring and automated maintenance keep systems running at peak performance.

  • Proactive Issue Prevention: Weekly checks and real-time alerts help mitigate risks before they escalate.

  • Cross-Functional Benefits: Whether it’s integration flows, BW, or Fiori apps, a tailored gardener approach optimizes each area uniquely.

  • Enhanced ROI: Reduced downtime and manual intervention translate into significant cost savings and better resource allocation.

Next Steps 🚀

  1. Reflect: Consider which areas of your SAP ecosystem could benefit most from a dedicated "gardener" approach.

  2. Experiment: Start with one module (e.g., Integration Flow Gardener) to test the concept in a controlled environment.

  3. Collaborate: Share your experiences and insights with your team or on professional platforms like LinkedIn to learn and grow collectively.

  4. Innovate: Explore automation tools and best practices that can integrate seamlessly with your SAP systems, driving both efficiency and innovation.

entry.title: Saving / opening a model (PDM) takes far too long
entry.content:
Hi,

we have different sized PDMs organized in a project. The largest model file (BIN) is 39 MB and contains ~700 tables with over 20000 columns. It takes SAP PowerDesigner (PD) ~6 min to save the mode
But the time it takes for PD to save the model does not correlate with the size. 22 MB model took 2 min. A 27 MB model only took 1 min.

When opening the models, it also takes PD a very long time. We have an extreme case, where it takes ~40 min to open a 1 MB PDM with 50 tables and around 600 columns. The above mentioned biggest model in our project needs ~40 sec to open.

We observe no difference, when saving the models as XML instead of BIN.

Our Setup:
PD Version: 16.7.7.0
installed on Virtual Workstation with AMD EPYC 3 Ghz, 48 GB RAM

What is the problem here? What is the bottleneck?
With older versions of PD and similar sized models it was much faster (Unfortunately I cannot say exactly with which version, it was a gradual process).

Are there other users, who have the same or similar problem?
We would be happy about any suggestions / hints?

Thanks.

entry.title: Material Length - SAP Readiness Check
entry.content:
Hello,

The SAP Note 2215852 described how ABAP code should look in order to be consistent with the new material length. In the Note there is following code example:
"So the adapted code looks as follows:"

DATA: ls_matnr_selection TYPE bapimatram,
        lt_matnr_selection TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapimatram,
        lt_material_list   TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapimatlst,
        ls_material_list   TYPE bapimatlst.


  ls_matnr_selection-option         = 'CP'.
  ls_matnr_selection-sign           = 'I'.
  ls_matnr_selection-matnr_low_long = 'MAT_A*'.

  INSERT ls_matnr_selection INTO TABLE lt_matnr_selection.

  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_MATERIAL_GETLIST'
    TABLES
      matnrselection = lt_matnr_selection
      matnrlist      = lt_material_list.

  SORT lt_material_list BY material_long.

  READ TABLE lt_material_list INTO ls_material_list INDEX 1.

  DATA: ls_material_general_data TYPE bapimatdoa,
        lv_old_mat_no            TYPE bismt.

  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_MATERIAL_GET_DETAIL'
    EXPORTING
      material_long         = ls_material_list-material_long
    IMPORTING
      material_general_data = ls_material_general_data.

  lv_old_mat_no = ls_material_general_data-old_mat_no_long.

I have created a z-report with this and only this exact code and checked it with the Custom Code Analysis using Variant S4HANA_READINESS and I still receive the same findings (concerning lines 13 & 25) that one gets for unadapted code:

So SAP's suggested fix for these findings has no impact and since it is one of the most common findings, we need another way to fix them, ideally not through pragmas. Any suggestions are appreciated.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Model product Specification Odata service in ABAP Clo...
entry.content:
Hello @niranjan_vivekanandan,

The CDS View I_MODELPRODUCTSPECIFICATIONTP & I_ModProdSpecItemTP is created as Projection view and the system does not allow use of projection views as Base object. We recommend using the root view(R_ModelProductSpecificationTP,R_ModProdSpecItemTP ) to create another entity/projection. Hope this helps.

Best Regards,

Maciej

entry.title: RAP OData v4 Web-API media handling / streams / file up-/download
entry.content:
Hello everybody,

I searched in help portal, community and learning to get some information about media handling, OData streams, file transfer or whatever name someone gives it. I found several examples, including RAP100 learning story and the help article, but all of these use UI services with draft handling. Hence I am developing a web api on an 2023 onPremise release I wanted to ask if anyone knows an example or a guide for web api implementation of media handling.

I think the most probable scenario for me would be to use a custom entity if it is possible.

Does anyone know something about this topic?

entry.title: Re: Help with setup of SAP Ariba Buying, Base Edition (Scope Item 4AI)
entry.content: Hi Lukas, do you know who it is works with this missing entitlements. I have the same issue. Do we need a extra licence here although SAP says that we only need the SAP Public Cloud Edition licence.

entry.title: Re: How to get registered object information from an ABAP...
entry.content:
@MegumiInoue 

The only CDS view I know remotely applicable to your question is I_CustABAPObjDirectoryEntry. At least you get the list of TABL.

Don't know about other CDS views. You can search in ADT with for example Open ABAP Development Object -> api:use_in_cloud_development type:ddls *tab*

entry.title: Re: Yard Logistics: Auto-Generate Opposite Yard Order doe...
entry.content:
Hej Ajit,

many thanks for your reply.

I did some more research in the SPPFP and I can't see actions for /SAPYL/YO_YR_GENERATE_OPOS_DIR. Looks like they aren't triggered anymore. Did some research in SPRO (Cross-Application Components > Processes and Tools for Enterprise Applications > Reusable Objects and Functions for BOPF Environment > Maintain PPF Settings)  if the Action Profile, Action Definition and Types of Processing are there anymore. They are, so I compared a system with the old status with my system with a new status and found one difference. 

When it comes to the processing types the system says, that "The processing settings could not be loaded." I think here is a problem. 

Many Greetings,

Sandra

entry.title: Re: Branch field name in VPM2
entry.content:
Hi ramco1917,

The field named as "Branch" in the rows (either Payment Invoice rows or Account rows) is for view/reference only, it is not stored in database.

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: New Pipeline Concept features: integrated runtimes and supporting landscape stages
entry.content:
@Benko this is a bug of the handled by integration flow feature, I have reported an internal incident with high prio, development are working on fixing it and will patch the tenants soon, as long as this hasn't been fixed you may revert to version 1.0.2 of the generic XI inbound flow, version 1.0.2 however would then only work for async scenarios

Alex

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 SAP Cloud Application Programming Model with SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Do we get any confirmation mail?

entry.title: Re: SAP Marketing Cloud Release 2402 is Here!
entry.content:
@vervinckt_joyca Yes, pretty strange, but it seems that these fields are not available in segmentation configuration 🤔

In the contact interaction timeline you'll have to make the fields visible using the Adapt UI option and you should see these fields there:

The fields should also be available in at least view I_MKT_INTERACTION and can be read using API_MKT_INTERACTION for example.

entry.title: Re: SAP Gardener: Cultivating a Thriving Digital Ecosystem 🌱🚀

very interesting post, thank you.

Kind regards, Sven

entry.title: Exception during tokenizer creation with message 'Forbidden character in state PHRASE->['.
entry.content: hello, everybody Now I have a problem Our team developed a cloud application using sap cap framework, deployed it on btp, used the odata V4 generated by CDS for communication, and used UI5 element to generate some pages at the front-end. When executing the global search of odata, an error occurred: Exception during tokenizer creation with message 'Forbidden character in state PHRASE->['. It should be noted that only some special character searches will result in errors Do you know how to fix this?

entry.title: Explore Netherlands EPR Declaration for 2024 with SAP Responsible Design and Production
entry.content:
We are thrilled to introduce a new report category in SAP Responsible Design and Production to support the mandate for Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) reporting for Netherlands in 2024. This update ensures compliance with the latest regulatory requirements announced by Verpact.

 

SAP’s Circular Economy Vision

SAP is committed to transforming businesses and value chains for a circular economy, aiming for a future with zero waste. Our technology focuses on:

  • Eliminating waste and pollution
  • Circulating materials and keeping products in use
  • Regenerating natural systems

For more information, see Circular Economy Solutions. 

 

SAP Responsible Design and Production

SAP Responsible Design and Production provides intelligence that allows your company to monitor, measure, and act, so you can eliminate waste and circulate materials. SAP Responsible Design and Production calculates EPR obligations, plastic taxes, and corporate commitments to optimize material choices, reduce fees, and reduce risks coming from unforeseen regulatory costs and non-compliance. It enables you to make effective decisions with strategic data insights and analytics. 

For more information on the product or to request a demo, access SAP Responsible Design and Production.

 

Key Updates in the 2024 Netherlands EPR Regulation

The Netherlands has introduced significant changes to its EPR reporting requirements for 2024, affecting packaging producers and importers. Key changes include the following:

  • Updated Reporting Structure: The structure and format of EPR reports have been modified to align with the new regulatory framework, enabling you to keep up with the changing EU legislations.
  • New Packaging Categories: Additional types of packaging have been introduced as relevant for reporting, such as reusable cups and plastic bottles without deposit.
  • Enhanced Single-Use Plastic (SUP) Determination Logic: A revised methodology to identify and categorize single-use plastics for more accurate reporting.
  • Fee Modulation: New logic for discounts based on packaging material and recyclability.
  • Changes to EPR Fees for Packaging: Modifications affecting cost calculations for producers.

 

How SAP Responsible Design and Production Supports Compliance

Our new report category offers the following:

  • Automated Compliance Reporting: Generates reports adhering to the new mandate.
  • Customizable Data Inputs: Adapts reporting parameters based on business-specific packaging data.
  • Accurate Fee Calculation: Applies the latest EPR fee structures and modulations.
  • Enhanced Data Validation: Minimizes errors with built-in validation mechanisms.

 

For more details on this update and implementation, see Change in tariffs for packaging data reporting in 2024 in the Regulatory Change Manager, refer to our official documentation, or contact your account executive.

 

 

There are many features and functions which have been released in SAP Responsible Design and Production. You can check the What’s New section in the SAP Help Portal to know the latest updates for every new release.

For an outlook on upcoming regulatory changes and features, you can consult the Regulatory Change Manager and the SAP Road Map Explorer for SAP Responsible Design and Production. 

Stay up to date with EPR mandates and plastic taxes with SAP Responsible Design and Production!

entry.title: Re: New End User File Upload in SAP Analytics Cloud QRC3 2024
entry.content:
Hello all,

Is there any plan for when the master data functionality will come up?

This feature is a must for Operational Workforce Planning since we have planners who want to upload positions in bulk. Since the application has embedded master data creation, it becomes impossible to leverage flat file capabilities.

@gerd_schoeffl I hope it becomes a priority, even if it has restricted features.

BR,
Miguel

entry.title: Request for Script to Control Measure in SAP Analytics Cloud Table Using Range Slide
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working on an analytic application in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) using SAP BW live data. I would like to control a measure in a table using a range slider through scripting. Specifically, I want to dynamically filter the measure based on the values selected in the range slider.

Requirements:

Range Slider: I have a range slider component (RangeSlider_1) in my application.

Table: I have a table component (Table_1) that displays data from SAP BW.

Dynamic Filtering: The measure in the table should be filtered dynamically based on the range slider's values.

Request:

Could you please provide a script

entry.title: Re: find me a bapi/fm to change a order for iw32 t-code
entry.content:
Hi Vikas,

all tags you added, are low level technical components (RFC and network communication). No one from these areas will be able to answer the question. (There are millions of BAPIs/FMs.) You will need to check, from which application component IW32 is, and then add a matching tag for this application.

entry.title: Re: Exception during tokenizer creation with message 'Forbidden character in state PHRASE->['.
entry.content: Supplement: The essence of the problem is that when performing a search for something like a special character, an error is reported directly /api/sisl/OperatedProducts? $search="["

entry.title: Re: Compensation – EC Entry Level Field Labels Not Visible
entry.content: @xavierlegarrec It worked! Thank you for your help! I also tested the settings separately, and the key setting for it to work is 'translatable'!

entry.title: Re: Could not determine EWM stock data for: Storage Locat...
entry.content:
Hi @akira887 ,

Could you please share master data screen shot seems something wrong in that please check Source Storage location, Warehouse process type ....enterexception...etc

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/49d0972a760b4646918ce9fddb034d64/b89038c4f5db4d8cab8696d9adbe0975.html?locale=en-US

 

Source link-https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/49d0972a760b4646918ce9fddb034d64/b89038c4f5db4d8cab8696d9adbe0975.html?locale=en-US

You have created a Kanban Control Cycle. In the control cycle, you have made the following settings on the Stock Transfer tab page: Stock transfer strategy: Enter either the standard strategy Warehouse Task (EWM), or a strategy with the control type Stock Transfer with Warehouse Task. Storage location (source): You must enter an EWM-managed source storage location. Enter the destination storage bin and the warehouse process type. In case of a IM-managed target, the destination bin is an intermediate bin. In case of an EWM-managed target, the bin is part of the warehouse related to the storage location of the PSA. The warehouse process type should be configured for stock transfer to production supply. Note In the settings of the warehouse process type, it is possible to define a default exception code for this replenishment process. Whenever a kanban container is set to full with a quantity that differs from the requested quantity, the corresponding warehouse task is confirmed with a difference using this exception code.

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Re: How to reach out to SAP Education - Learning Hub team for support
entry.content: Dear @SAP_Krish I am sorry for the delay. You will need to create a case for the Learning team to provide assistance on this matter. In case that you're having issues with the case creation please contact us at CIC via Phone, Email or Chat and we will be happy to assist you.

entry.title: transaction OITA and assets under construction
entry.content:
Hi,

in OITA you define a investmentprofile with default assets under construction class. In V_T350_IV you assign the investment profile to a (PM-CS)-order class. When the order is released, the AuC is created.

in OITA

There's also the flag "fixed default class" where the help says "

Asset class for assets u. construction is not changeable

Use

Determines how the asset class can be entered for the asset under construction that belongs to an investment measure, when you create the investment measure. If this indicator is set, the asset class cannot be entered manually. The system then uses the asset class entered in the investment profile."

Am I wrong to assume that when the flag is not set, you should be able to choose your AuC-class when creating the asset? How can that be accomplished?

kr

Nico

entry.title: Re: How to reach out to SAP Education - Learning Hub team for support
entry.content:
Dear @Leonardo_Martinez 

Could you please contact us at CIC via Phone, Email or Chat and we will be happy to assist you. We will need your case ID number and user details to follow up with the Learning team on this.

entry.title: How to check the retention time when delete the EWA sessions?
entry.content:
How to check the retention time for a system when I use report RDSMOPREDUCEDATA to delete the EWA sessions?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to check the retention time when delete the EWA s...
entry.content:
Normally, the retention times for a system are found in transaction SOLMAN_EWA_ADMIN.
If you are using a specific variant to run the report RDSMOPREDUCEDATA to delete EWA sessions, you can check the variant details in SE38->RDSMOPREDUCEDATA.
There is one option "Retain sessions from the Last  () days" , it has the same meaning of retention times.
Refer SAP KBA:2427041 - How to delete old EWA sessions according to retention times automatically through a job

entry.title: Re: Bapi for delivery split and batch change
entry.content: Normally, automatic batch determination will happen in delivery and if you don't want, it can be changed manually.  This being the case, what is the Business requirement and is it that in all line items in delivery, batch number to be changed ?

entry.title: Re: restrict interviewers to edit candidate rating
entry.content: the interviewer still can edit rating in all statuses as a result

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA vs. ECC : Key Changes Every Security Consultant Should Know
entry.content:
As a security consultant, not a functional expert, I’m outlining the key differences between SAP S/4HANA and SAP ECC. This overview highlights important changes that are useful when handling security-related tasks.

1st is Business Partners.

BP/Business Partners is not a new concept, it was there in ECC system as well, I remember using it for a particular CRM implementation, where we had multiple relationships to be defined for external partners. But most of the companies still relied on maintaining vendor and customer master data separately. Now with S/4 all the master data is maintained in BP. If you ever do the brownfield implementation, you will see XK01 etc (vendor and customer master data related) tcodes are obsolete and SU25 will suggest to replace it with tcode BP.

This goes with the fact that an external entity can have multiple relations with a business. For instance, Microsoft may act as a vendor for SAP by providing Office 365 solutions while also being a customer using SAP’s ERP system. In Business Partner (BP), these two roles are defined separately, but the core master data for Microsoft remains centralized, ensuring consistency across different relationships.

2nd is Lesser no of Tables

SAP S/4HANA has a reduced number of tables compared to the ECC system, but this applies specifically to the S/4HANA solution, not the HANA database itself. If an ECC system is connected to a HANA database, it will still retain the larger number of tables from ECC.

For example, FI and CO tables have been consolidated into a single table, ACDOCA (Universal Journal), serving as a single source of truth. Additionally, summary tables have been eliminated due to HANA’s column level in-memory storage, enabling faster analytical processing.

3rd is Universal Journal – ACDOCA (Single source of truth)

In ECC solution, FI (Financial Accounting module) and CO (Controlling/Management Accounting) used to have different tables. With S/4HANA, all accounting related postings are posted in one single table called ACDOCA, also called universal journal. Within the same entry posted to ACDOCA, amount can be split and be associated with different cost/profit centers.

4th is Material ledger

If companies are using S/4HANA for their inventory management, using material Ledger is a must. Material ledger is just like BP, which was there in ECC system, but companies didn’t use it much. The material ledger in S/4HANA supports up to 3 currencies.

5th is Foreign Trade

The foreign trade functionality now cannot be used in FI module. If company implementing S/4HANA is into foreign trade operations, they would need to implement SAP GTS. (Global Trade Services).

6th is Embedded Analytics

SAP S/4HANA comes with Embedded Analytics, allowing companies to access real-time insights without needing a separate BW system—perfect for those who don’t require highly customized reports. Key benefits include:

  • Cost Saving - Reduced expenses on licenses, infrastructure, and maintenance.
  • Real-Time Data – Instant access to live business information without replication delays.
  • No Data Duplication – Eliminates redundancy by analyzing data directly within S/4HANA.

7th is Embedded GRC

Just like analytics, S/4HANA gives an option to install in embedded mode. Embedded GRC, just like embedded analytics have same pros – lesser cost, no data duplication and real time analysis. I will cover more about embedded GRC and how it differs from standalone GRC installation in my next blog.

 

I've covered the key points, though not everything. However, this should give security professionals a solid starting point for S/4HANA implementations and interviews

entry.title: Re: How to redefine RDS based OData services?
entry.content:
Hi @Andre_Fischer , 

we redefined RDS based odata service SLL_PROD_CMDTYCD_RECLASSIFY. however, there is no new CDS added to the extended service, only that we wanted few code changes in the DPC_EXT. since no new cds so the model exposure methods are not available in the custom extended class. the get_dpc is also not redefined. 

the app is working fine on the list page, but when we navigate to object page there is a fields for commodity code , the f4 help is not showing as it is showing int he standard service call. 

below screen which comes up when we click on the commodity code F4 in object page, is from standard service. in the extended these columns do not appear and it raise error "no columns is selected" where as the  value help cds C_CommodityCodeForKeyDateVH is visible in the custom service metadata file just as in the standard metadata . and when clicked on GO button it will raise value help cds C_CommodityCodeForKeyDateVH is not found.

 

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Cloud Integration - Configuring Scenario Using the XI Sender Adapter
entry.content:
Hi Mandy,

for a few days SAP published the new "Exclusive" access type feature for JMS queues to ensure EOIO between CPI middleware ->receiver interface communication part. 

In above link the author mentioned that XI sender adapter still does not support EOIO.
Is there a SAP plan when XI sender adapter will support EOIO to ensure that messages from ABAP sender are received in CPI in the origin order?
Only with this real End-to-End EOIO could be guaranteed as prerequisite that we could migrate our affected XI sender integration scenarios from legacy PO system.

Is maybe the new XI sender adapter option "handled by Integration Flow" exactly the solution to also guarantee EOIO in ABAP XI sender -> CPI middleware communication part in order to close the feature gap to old PO mechanism?

BR,
Tobias Miller

entry.title: Re: SAP Marketing Cloud Release 2402 is Here!
entry.content:
💡Thanks @MarkusGrant , with UI adaptation I can now see them on the interaction.

Can I also ask how you were able to use the fields in the LeadGen? I checked in CPI but in the mapping step for the Lead, I would have expected the utm fields to show up in the PredecessorInteraction field group where my custom fields on interaction appear, but alas. You mentioned an API, so I'm assuming that then within the CPI integration flow you make a call back to SMC to know the values for those UTM fields?

@BACKESSI mentioned a Badi in the post... it would be helpful if through a badi these seemingly elusive utm fields could be stored in actual custom fields that can be used in segmentation and in cpi mapping... 

UPDATE: custom logic CUAN_IA_REVISE_FOR_IMPORT has some promising mention of the utm fields in the badi documentation... I'll check that one out.

entry.title: Re: Top 10 SAP Cloud ALM News March 2025
entry.content:
Hello Martin,

This is already possible! We know the configured Transport Management System (TMS) routes and the transports can also be transported for 4-tier landscapes. The following slide illustrates it more detailed.

I hope this was helpful. 

All the best,

Marbeth

entry.title: Re: Compatibility Scope: What Happens after 2025/2030
entry.content:
Dear Hiroshi,
please let me answer both of your questions.
(1) Generally, your understanding is correct. As said in note 2269324 for SAP S/4HANA systems under a RISE with SAP agreement, the Compatibility Pack use right will expire Dec 31, 2030. For customers without a RISE agreement, the previous expiry dates stay in place.

(2) Regarding technical availability of compatibility package functionality, please refer to item 3 of this blog.
As said there, we reserves the right to make compatibility scope functionalities technically unavailable in future SAP S/4HANA releases. Decisions will be taken individually by each functionality. For release SAP S/4HANA 2025, for example, this will be the case for HCM as described in SAP note 3443536.
In already general available S/4HANA releases, we will not subsequently deactivate anything, though. Technical deactivation of individual functionalities in future releases will be reported via the existing simplification items and related notes. 

Regards,
Stefan

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Integration | SAP Concur
entry.content:
Hi  

Thank you for sharing!!

We got an error when posting an Intercompany employee expense. Employee is in company A but allocate the expense to company B and B cost center.

The error is like below, could you please help?

Create company code  or change your entry

Message no. F5165

Diagnosis

Company code  has not been created in financial accounting.

Procedure

Correct the entry or create the company code in Customizing for financial accounting.

entry.title: Re: Stay certified due
entry.content:
Hi @AX-76 
Please check our Troubleshooting SAP Universal ID  document.
Based on your case you find there guidance on steps you need to take in order to solve the issue.

If you are an SAP Partner,

  • Go to SAP Partner Portal
  • Access Contact Us from the menu on the right side
  • Choose "User Management" add the following information:
    • e-mail
    • S-User for the exam
    • Exam code
    • Proof of certification (PDF, screenshot, Credly URL)
    • SAP Universal ID

If you are an individual / SAP Customer, please create a case here

  • Select category "Learning and Certification Advice" and enter the following Information:
    • Email address
    • S-User taken for the exam
    • Exam Code
    • Proof of certification completion (by either pdf or screenshot or by Credly URL
    • SAP Universal ID Email address

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Adobe forms with fragments based on ODATA
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am new adobe forms. I want to explore Form with fragments having ODATA service. I have few doubts

- In SFP what is the interface name that we need to enter

- I don't see any field to enter ODATA service name

How do we create a Form with fragments from scratch? I only found tutorials on copying standard ODATA forms and making some changes but not really How-to tutorials for beginners

As a second option, I have copied a std Form with fragments and replaced standard odata service with a custom one. I have deleted all the elements from layout.  I have added a text element in the layout and I want to bind it to some odata entity property. In normal Adobe forms we can use context and bind it easily. In case of ODATA based form there is no context etc., so what is the easy way to do the binding. It is really difficult to type in all the ODATA entity path for each of the object in the layout. I am sure there must be a easy way

SAP Forms service by Adobe SAP NetWeaver Application Server for ABAP NW ABAP Gateway (OData) @mmcisme1 Hemanshi17

entry.title: Xsteps in Process order. How to add custom logic to select needed SXS references in XSTEP
entry.content:
Dear all,

it would be great if someone could share some thoughts on how to approach the following regarding Xsteps in PO.

we have Process orders and Xsteps. In Xsteps we have one node (step) with two different alternative SXS references. This is done so in order to let user select which one of those 2 will be used in Control Recipe.

The target is to add custom logic during Control Recipe generaion or during Process order changes (save, release, etc) so that depending on which Resource is selected in operation assigned to that Xstep to have one or another SXS reference selected automatically. Basically i'm looking for some method (Badi, exit, anything else) that could be used in Process order to enfluence selecting one of 2 SXS references.

I'm aware of two main directions of BADI enhancements: sybmol valuation and scope generation. However, i'm doubting how one of those 2 could be used for my case...

Many thanks in advance

KR, Andrew

entry.title: Are Signavio Value Accelerators available in other languages than English?
entry.content:
We are using the following Value Accelerator in Signavio: Process design and standardization for SAP SuccessFactors.

Does anyone knows if it is available in any other languages than English? E.g. French?

I'm referring to the content of the V.A.: the value chains, best practices, processes, etc.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Could not determine EWM stock data for: Storage Locat...

entry.title: Re: SAP Marketing Cloud Release 2402 is Here!
entry.content:
@vervinckt_joyca The final solution will of course depend a bit on what you want to do with the data, but you can use the Custom Logic "Adjust Interaction" (CUAN_IA_REVISE_FOR_IMPORT) to copy the UTM parameter(s) from predecessor interaction for example so it's stored on the lead interaction or other interaction triggered by the form fillout.

And as you mention the UTM parameters are unfortunately not sent out in the PredecessorInteraction part of the lead payload, so you could do an additional call back to MC from CPI like you mention to fetch the parameter(s) that you need.

entry.title: How can I change the name of BP
entry.content:
Dear Community, 

I cannot change the name of a business partner (supplier and employee). Where do I have to adjust the settings to make this possible?
Thank you very much for your support.

Best regards,

Güli

entry.title: Is it possible to assess HR processes with SAP Signavio Process Insights without Success Factor?
entry.content: We would like to improve our HR processes with the support of SAP Signavio Process Insight. We haven't migrated to S4 HANA nor implemented Success Factor yet, but use SAP ECC for certain transactions related to HR. Is there anything: Dashboards, KPIs, etc. related to HR available?

entry.title: Re: There is an error when receiving a VAT Invoice number
entry.content: You do not have permissions to access this page.

entry.title: Re: How to select the status of declaration in app "Manage Input VAT Declaration - China"?
entry.content: You do not have permissions to access this page.

entry.title: Re: COND_COUNT - Cannot convert 123 into NUM: loss of sig...
entry.content:
The long field is available on my S/4HANA systems, but not on my ECC EHP7 (both were SP upgraded last year), and I haven't found any ECC-related notes.

  • So raise an issue with sap support with SD-SLS and mention the S/4HANA notes 
  • If unsuccessful, consider using Customer Influence to request this development to be downgraded to ECC

entry.title: Re: Bank accounts cannot be deleted
entry.content:
I have found the solution.
You cannot delete the business partner's bank details via the “Manage supplier master data” app. There is the app "Report for Removing Address and Bank details from Business Partner".

entry.title: Re: SAP Marketing Cloud Release 2402 is Here!
entry.content:
Indeed @MarkusGrant , that custom logic seems to be what I need 🙂

For anyone still coming along this topic here in he future,

In the custom logic CUAN_IA_REVISE_FOR_IMPORT I added:

    IF interaction_data-utm_source IS NOT INITIAL.
        interaction_data-yy1_ga_source_mia = interaction_data-utm_source.
    ENDIF.

My custom field YY1_GA_SOURCE_MIA, in the tab SOAP APIs can be enabled for LeadReplicationRequest_Out. And that will make it appear in the PredecessorInteraction part in the mapping step in CPI. From there CPI can apply the logics that we need. Plus we can use the YY1_GA_SOURCE_MIA field directly in segmentation, where we actually do most of our "reporting" too, i.e. to see how many incoming interactions came from which source, and how many of those prospects eventually became customers.

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 SAP Cloud Application Programming Model with SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content: Yes we will send confirmation mail to first 35 registered participant’s

We tried as said Step 2 above. 
Will the dependency name change for SAP BAS?
In the drop down we don't see Build Apps but we do see BAS.
Please do suggest as we are unable to access multiple CAP applications from Work Zone and they have same 500 error.
Thank you.
@EsmeeX

entry.title: Re: @sap/datasphere-cli: Command-Line Interface for SAP Datasphere: Overview
entry.content:
Hello, 

How can we create replication flows through CLI? Suppose I have more than 100 pipelines which I need to migrate from DI? Rather than creating each replication flow one by one, can I do it using CLI as there is no import option (CSN/JSON) yet for replication flows!

entry.title: New SAP LeanIX adapter for SAP Integration Suite is available!
entry.content:
Motivation

To simplify the integration with SAP LeanIX, we created an Standard adapter for SAP Integration Suite.

The current features are:

  • the adapter can use the GraphQL and REST API endpoints of SAP LeanIX
  • read data from SAP LeanIX workspaces for further processing in Integration Suite
  • Update data in SAP LeanIX with an easy-to-use UI in the adapter config

Some benefits for customer could be:

  • Integrate SAP LeanIX more seamlessly with other SAP and Non-SAP solutions
  • Give also Low- and NoCode developer an easy way to get data in and out of SAP LeanIX
  • Easily use and adapt our standard integration scenarios through SAP standard content

Architecture overview

The adapter is available via SAP Business Accelerator Hub. There is no option to use it as sender adapter (see next chapter), but as receiver adapter for GraphQL and REST interface. It also covers the login to SAP LeanIX in an easy way. You can use predefined REST or GraphQL queries directly via the adapter config or build you custom GraphQL statements.  

Sender Scenario

To send data from SAP LeanIX to another system, you could use WebHooks: How that could be done, is described here. To use this data in SAP Integration Suite, you can create an Integration Flow with a HTTP Sender adapter. For authorization purpose, we have a option for basic auth and OAuth2 in SAP LeanIX for WebHooks.

 

Features

Simple Integration Flow

GraphQL Protocol

  • Query and manipulate Fact Sheet via GraphQL
  • Pass created queries or use adapter feature to build your query
  • Full coverage of LeanIX GraphQL API
  • Security Handling for connecting to LeanIX

REST Protocol

  • Build REST queries for LeanIX a No-Code way
  • Full coverage of LeanIX REST API with dependencies and parameter
  • Security Handling for connecting to SAP LeanIX

Outlook

With the release of the SAP LeanIX adapter, we delivered some basic features to make integrations easier for developers. What do you think, what could be helpful? What would be scenarios, you would like to implement in SAP Integration Suite?

Here are some ideas, where I would like to get feedback from you, if that would be helpful?

For the SAP LeanIX adapter:

  • Add transactional logic
  • Enable extended exception handling
  • Flexible GraphQL query builder
  • Mass operations
  • better Support for custom Fact Sheets

For potential Integration Content packages:

  • Integration with more SAP Cloud Solutions?
  • Custom Integrations with SAP Signavio and SAP CALM?
  • Custom Integrations with other CMDB and project management solutions etc.?

entry.title: Re: How to hide Business partner description.
entry.content: Thank you for sharing with us

entry.title: ValueHelp with parameters where the value help provider entity is a custom entity with parameters
entry.content:
Hello, 

I am trying to use a valueHelp for which the value help provider entity is a custom entity with parameters for which I will have a class that implements a custom query in an ABAP RAP app. 

Here is my custom entity: 

@EndUserText.label: 'Value Help Entity for Appointment Slots'

@ObjectModel.query.implementedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_APPOINTMENT_SLOTS_QUERY'

@odata.entityType.name: 'ZVH_HA_APPOIN_SLOTSParameters'

@odata.entitySet.name: 'ZVH_HA_APPOIN_SLOTS'

define custom entity ZVH_HA_APPOIN_SLOTS

with parameters

appointmentDate : zha_date_dats,

doctorId: zha_doctor_id

{

key appointTime: zha_time;

}

 

Here is the valueHelp Annotation:

 

@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{

entity: {name: 'ZVH_HA_APPOIN_SLOTS', element: 'appointTime'},

additionalBinding: [

{ parameter:'appointmentDate' , localElement: 'AppointmentDate' },

{ parameter: 'doctorId', localElement:'DoctorId' }

]

}]

AppointmentHour,

 The custom view is exposed in service:

@EndUserText: {

label: 'Service Definition for ZC_HA_APPOIN'

}

@ObjectModel: {

leadingEntity: {

name: 'ZC_HA_APPOIN'

}

}

define service ZUI_HA_APPOIN_O4

provider contracts odata_v4_ui {

expose ZC_HA_APPOIN as Appoint;

expose ZC_HA_APP_MS as AppMS;

expose ZC_HA_DOCUMENTS_APPOIN as Documents;

expose ZVH_HA_APPOIN_SLOTS;

}

When I try to click on the field AppointmentHour in UI (in preview) I get error in the browser console and I get stuck there:

 

2025-03-27 12:27:36.371000 Unknown child ZVH_HA_APPOIN_SLOTSSet of com.sap.gateway.srvd_f4.zvh_ha_appoin_slots.v0001.Container - /ZVH_HA_APPOIN_SLOTSSet/ sap.ui.model.odata.v4.ODataMetaModel

2025-03-27 12:27:36.372000 Inconsistent value help metadata: Entity /ZVH_HA_APPOIN_SLOTSSet is not defined -

2025-03-27 12:27:36.372000 selectedInfo or selectedContent undefined -

 

I do not arrive with the flow in the class : zcl_appointment_slots_query because it stops at this error.

 

I have attached also a screen shot with the error.

 

Would you please help?

 

Thank you

 

Regards

Bogdan

entry.title: Re: Purchased & Assigned SAP Learning Hub Subscription to do stay current not working or inactiv
entry.content: Hi Margit, I create the support ticket 2025-03-25 and updated the ticket yesterday after I posted my question in the SAP Community. In addition, I escalated my support request this morning with the help of a Customer Interaction Center contact. I then received the following answer: As your license is a subscription for two attempts on SAP Certification Hub, the A_ACT_2404 (assessment) is not included. With the two attempts it is possible to take the re-certification (C_ACT). Ensure you have an SAP Learning Hub subscription to access the assessment to stay certified. My quest is now Can the license I bought (SAP Certification exam, two attempts) be converted to cover A_ACT_2404 or do I have to purchase another license? If I need to purchase another/new license, what license will be needed to cover the A_ACT_2404 (assessment)? Kind regards Armin

entry.title: Consumption in bill of material
entry.content:
Hi all,

could you please advise for following issue which we are facing. We are packing finished goods into trays, we need 2 trays for 10 pieces of finish goods(lets say top and bottom). In BOM we have set up base quantity 10 pieces, for mentioned raw material is component quantity 2 pieces.

Logic which is used in reality is like this:

  • Producing 1-10 pieces of finished goods use 2 pieces of raw material.
  • Producing 11-20 pieces of finished goods use 4 pieces of raw material.
  • Producing 21-30 pieces of finished goods use 6 pieces of raw material, and so on.

Issue is that SAP is not calculating "low quantities" correctly. When we are production 1-5 pieces, system calculate only 1 piece of tray, which is not correct - we still use 2 trays.

We cannot use fixed quantity because if we are producing for example 10-15pieces for which we use 4 trays(10 pieces FG =2 trays plus another 5 pieces FG= 2 trays), with fixed quantity it would be always 2.

Also create another alternative BOMs and then another production version based on lot size is not the correct way for us, there would be a lot of prod. versions.

Could you please advise if it is possible make setup which will reflect calculation above?

entry.title: Re: COND_COUNT - Cannot convert 123 into NUM: loss of sig...
entry.content: Ok, I see. Thank you for the information!

entry.title: Re: operational reporting for procurement synchronous api
entry.content: Hallo Siruiliu. Great and Thanks for the input, I just framed custom template. will this suffice to fetch single invoice or multiple invoice report.

entry.title: Re: operational reporting for procurement synchronous api
entry.content: { "documentType":"Invoice" "status":"Approved","Rejected","Paid" "selectAttributes":["InvoceId"], "filterExpressions" : [ { "field" : "TimeUpdated", "op" : "<=", "defaultValue" : "2025-03-31T00:00:00Z", "name" : "updatedDateTo" }, { "field" : "TimeUpdated", "op" : ">", "defaultValue" : "2025-03-001T00:00:00Z", "name" : "updatedDateFrom" } ] }
